speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: Now, darting Parthia, art thou struck, and now

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: Pleased Fortune does of Marcus Crassus’ death

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: Make me revenger. Bear the King’s son’s body

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: Before our army. Thy Pacorus, Orodes,

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: Pays this for Marcus Crassus.

speaker: SILIUS
text: Noble Ventidius,

speaker: SILIUS
text: Whilst yet with Parthian blood thy sword is warm,

speaker: SILIUS
text: The fugitive Parthians follow. Spur through Media,

speaker: SILIUS
text: Mesopotamia, and the shelters whither

speaker: SILIUS
text: The routed fly. So thy grand captain, Antony,

speaker: SILIUS
text: Shall set thee on triumphant chariots and

speaker: SILIUS
text: Put garlands on thy head.

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: O, Silius, Silius,

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: I have done enough. A lower place, note well,

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: May make too great an act. For learn this, Silius:

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: Better to leave undone than by our deed

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: Acquire too high a fame when him we serve ’s away.

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: Caesar and Antony have ever won

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: More in their officer than person. Sossius,

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: One of my place in Syria, his lieutenant,

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: For quick accumulation of renown,

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: Which he achieved by th’ minute, lost his favor.

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: Who does i’ th’ wars more than his captain can

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: Becomes his captain’s captain; and ambition,

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: The soldier’s virtue, rather makes choice of loss

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: Than gain which darkens him.

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: I could do more to do Antonius good,

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: But ’twould offend him. And in his offense

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: Should my performance perish.

speaker: SILIUS
text: Thou hast, Ventidius, that

speaker: SILIUS
text: Without the which a soldier and his sword

speaker: SILIUS
text: Grants scarce distinction. Thou wilt write to Antony?

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: I’ll humbly signify what in his name,

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: That magical word of war, we have effected;

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: How, with his banners and his well-paid ranks,

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: The ne’er-yet-beaten horse of Parthia

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: We have jaded out o’ th’ field.

speaker: SILIUS
text: Where is he now?

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: He purposeth to Athens, whither, with what haste

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: The weight we must convey with ’s will permit,

speaker: VENTIDIUS
text: We shall appear before him.—On there, pass along!
